title: Lock package-manager local host as first harness target
rationale: The first harness needs a concrete protected action boundary and must not drift into host-wide or x402-centered claims.
acceptance: package-manager local host is named as the first target
package-install conformance is documented as a precondition
x402 is documented as calibration only
host-wide and ecosystem claims are explicitly excluded

title: Define HostFixtureManifest v1
rationale: Posture cannot be derived until host identity, protected path, wrapper, raw siblings, freshness, redaction, and non-claims are bound.
acceptance: manifest binds host identity, environment, adapter version, protected action type, protected path, wrapper path, raw siblings, registry entry, policy identity, freshness, tool digest, expected posture, redaction, and non-claims
missing required fields fail closed
manifest language states fixture-specific scope

title: Create package-install host fixture seed
rationale: The manifest needs concrete package-install fixture data before meaningful wrapped and raw sibling probes can run.
acceptance: fixture names one package-install protected path
fixture names configured wrapper and resolved path
fixture names raw sibling package-manager routes
fixture includes freshness timestamp and tool list digest
fixture includes explicit non-claims

title: Prove wrapped path one-use greenlight behavior
rationale: A harness must prove that the protected path executes only through an exact one-use gateway-bound greenlight.
acceptance: wrapped package-install attempt receives one exact greenlight
greenlight binds to one action contract
gateway check occurs before mutation
reuse of the greenlight is refused
gateway check and downstream result are recorded separately

title: Add raw sibling package-manager bypass probes
rationale: Wrapped success is meaningless if raw sibling mutation can bypass the contract boundary.
acceptance: each named raw sibling route is attempted or proof-gapped
raw sibling mutation possibility blocks READY
raw sibling refusal records evidence
raw sibling probe class is recorded in proof packet inputs

title: Add wrapper integrity and gateway binding checks
rationale: A configured wrapper path is not authority unless it resolves to the expected gateway-bound wrapper at freshness time.
acceptance: missing wrapper exits WRAPPER_MISSING
digest drift exits WRAPPER_DRIFTED
missing gateway binding exits GATEWAY_UNBOUND
resolved path and adapter version are recorded

title: Implement stale metadata refusal and proof-gap rules
rationale: Old probe success cannot authorize current posture after tool list, host digest, wrapper, or registry drift.
acceptance: stale probe exits STALE_PROBE
changed host tool digest exits HOST_TOOL_DIGEST_CHANGED
missing freshness exits PROOF_GAP
stale metadata cannot produce READY

title: Define BypassProofPacket and redacted projection
rationale: Reconstruction requires one evidence object that preserves gateway, downstream, refusal, bypass, freshness, and proof-gap distinctions.
acceptance: packet includes manifest reference and probe results
packet separates gateway check from downstream execution
packet records refusal, bypass, and proof gaps separately
redacted projection hides secrets without erasing control distinctions
non-claims are included

title: Derive protected-path posture from manifest and proof packet
rationale: Posture must be computed from evidence, not asserted by adapter optimism.
acceptance: READY requires fresh manifest, wrapper integrity, gateway binding, one-use greenlight, and no raw sibling mutation evidence
ADVISORY_ONLY is used for non-blocking surfaces
all required exit states are represented
posture is fixture-specific and never host-wide

title: Specify CLI report shape for harness evidence
rationale: Adoption starts with a report that makes evidence and non-claims explicit before dashboard work creates review theatre risk.
acceptance: report shows protected path, tool list digest, wrapper integrity, gateway binding, probe freshness, raw sibling attempts, evidence, and non-claims
report supports READY, ADVISORY_ONLY, WRAPPER_MISSING, WRAPPER_DRIFTED, GATEWAY_UNBOUND, RAW_SIBLING_MUTATION_POSSIBLE, STALE_PROBE, HOST_TOOL_DIGEST_CHANGED, and PROOF_GAP
report does not claim enforcement without blocking gateway checks

title: Add claim guards and closeout validation for harness scope
rationale: The hardest failure is a correct fixture result turned into an incorrect product claim.
acceptance: claim guards reject host-wide, platform-wide, package-manager-wide, MCP-wide, browser shell network, x402 ecosystem, and engineering-agent-only claims
quality and repo checks are documented as closeout gates
validation includes non-claim report checks
non_goals: turn planning labels into source API names
